pairs.question: What awards has Demetria Renee Tucker received for her work in youth programming?
pairs.answer: Demetria Renee Tucker received the 2013 National Arts and Humanities Youth Program Award at the White House for her work with the Pearl Bailey Library Youth Program. She also won the Virginia Hamilton Award for Lifetime Achievement from the American Library Association in the same year.

pairs.question: What is Demetria Renee Tucker's professional title and current workplace?
pairs.answer: Demetria Renee Tucker is the Senior Family and Youth Librarian at the Newport News Public Library.

pairs.question: What educational background does Demetria Renee Tucker have?
pairs.answer: Tucker earned a BA in Sociology from North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University in 1972 and an MLIS from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill School of Information and Library Science in 1978. She was later awarded the school's Distinguished Alumna Award in 2014.

pairs.question: What notable positions did Demetria Renee Tucker hold before her current role?
pairs.answer: Before her current role, Tucker worked for the Roanoke public library system from 1972 to 2004, then transitioned to the City of Roanoke Public Schools. She has been at the Newport News Public Library since 2008.

pairs.question: What role did Demetria Renee Tucker play in the Pearl Bailey Library Youth Program?
pairs.answer: Tucker designed the Pearl Bailey Library Youth Program to expand youth knowledge of literature and engage them as stakeholders in the library. Her work earned her the 2013 National Arts and Humanities Youth Program Award.

pairs.question: Can you provide a brief biography of Demetria Renee Tucker's early life and family?
pairs.answer: Demetria Renee Tucker was born in Greenville, North Carolina, to William A. Tucker and Helen Hemby Tucker. She had four siblings and is a member of the Lambda Omega chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha sorority.
